zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Sisson於1784年10月24日出生在加拿大新布隆士威省約克道格拉斯時，她的父親Jonathan Sisson22歲，母親Hulda Herrick19歲。她和Thomas William White至少有6個兒子和10個女兒。她去世於1857年7月9日在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓享年72歲，埋葬在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓。

nameMeanings.name: Sisson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English female personal name Sis(se), Cisse, a pet form of Cecily (see Sisley ) + -son.
variant of Syson, a habitational name from Syston (Leicestershire), from a short form of an Old English personal name such as Sigehǣth or Sigefrith + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Sissons
meanings: variant of Sisson , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Shays' Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Caused by war veteran Daniel Shays, Shays' Rebellion was to protest economic and civil rights injustices that he and other farmers were seeing after the Revolutionary War. Because of the Rebellion it opened the eyes of the governing officials that the Articles of Confederation needed a reform. The Rebellion served as a guardrail when helping reform the United States Constitution.

historicEvents.title: The Making of the U.S. Constitution.
historicEvents.description: The Philadelphia Convention was intended to be the first meeting to establish the first system of government under the Articles of Confederation. From this Convention, the Constitution of the United States was made and then put into place making it one of the major events in all American History.
Lewis and Clark set out from St. Louis, MO to explore the West.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.
Historical Boundaries: 1824: Washington, Maine, United States 1839: Aroostook, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Webster–Ashburton Treaty
historicEvents.description: The Webster-Ashburton Treaty was signed on August 9, 1842 and resolved the border issues between the United States and British North American colonies which had caused the Aroostook War. The treaty contained several agreements and concessions. It called for an end on the overseas slave trade and proposed that both parties share the Great Lakes. It also reaffirmed the location of the westward frontier border (near the Rocky Mountains) as well as the border between Lake Superior and Lake of the Woods. The treaty was signed by Daniel Webster (United States Secretary of State) and Alexander Baring (British Diplomat, 1st Baron Ashburton).
historicEvents.urlTitle: Office of the Historian - Webster-Ashburton Treaty, 1842

historicEvents.description: U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Sisson was born on 24 October 1784, in Douglas, York, New Brunswick, Canada, her father, Jonathan Sisson, was 22 and her mother, Hulda Herrick, was 19. She had at least 6 sons and 10 daughters with Thomas William White. She died on 9 July 1857, in Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, United States, at the age of 72, and was buried in Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Sisson est née le 24 octobre 1784, à Douglas, York, Nouveau-Brunswick, Canada, son père, Jonathan Sisson, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Hulda Herrick, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 10 filles avec Thomas William White. Elle est décédée le 9 juillet 1857, à Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Sisson(이)가 1784년 10월 24일 캐나다 뉴브런즈윅 요르크 더글라스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jonathan Sisson(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hulda Herrick(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Thomas William White(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1857년 7월 9일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 호지던에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 호지던에 묻혔다.

pt.24 October 1784–9 July 1857: 24 de outubro de 1784–9 de julho de 1857
pt.Keswick Ridge, Bright, York, New Brunswi: Keswick Ridge, Bright, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá
pt.York, New Brunswick, British Colonial Am: York, Novo Brunswick, British Colonial America

pt.Houlton, Aroostook, Maine, United States: Houlton, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Sisson nasceu em 24 de outubro de 1784, em Douglas, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá, seu pai, Jonathan Sisson, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Hulda Herrick, tinha 19 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 10 filhas com Thomas William White. Ela faleceu em 9 de julho de 1857, em Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Keswick Ridge, York, New Brunswick, Brit: кесвикк ридж, браит, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Sisson родилась 24 октября 1784 г. в местности доуглас, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада, её отцу по имени Jonathan Sisson было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Hulda Herrick, 19. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 10 дочерей от Thomas William White. Она умерла 9 июля 1857 г. в местности ходждон, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности ходждон, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Sisson nació el 24 de octubre de 1784, en Douglas, York, Nuevo Brunswick, Canadá, su papá, Jonathan Sisson, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Hulda Herrick, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 10 hijas con Thomas William White. Murió el 9 de julio de 1857, en Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos.
